reviews.review_text: More than just a zombie sandbox. This game offers a great story with unique characters alongside some rewarding gunplay.  Such a hidden gem!

While I understand why ppl would recommend and play this game, I felt as if the story suffered from strange dialogue and bad pacing. This story REALLY drags after a certain point and the side content really DOES NOT help, that at some point I just dropped this game altogether. The side content just boils down to clearing infected nests, hunting bounties, saving ppl from their own careless decisions, and clearing out bandit camps. That's about it. Deacon can kind of be polarizing at certain points of the story, but i will give him a pass since it is in post-apocalyptic world. I did not really care much for the love story that happened between Deacon and Sarah. Boozer is a great character though, and I really enjoyed the moments he was in the story. I stopped at the militia camp after "that" major discovery. Didn't feel compelled enough to continue further. Skizzo has gotta be one of the most obvious "good guy but eventually becomes a bad guy" character that i have ever seen in a video game. It would have been a way better twist if it had turned out he actually stayed a good guy to the end. Iron Mike is also a very hilariously bad, naive character. The game actively makes you go against his ideologies. The crafting kinda sucks, and the weapons can feel terrible against enemies at certain points. Making headshots the only viable way of dispatching enemies. The stealth is just "there," is the best way that i can describe it. You can be in absolute clear view and the enemies will either instantly notice you, or not at all, so it's very hit or miss. The bike combat is just bad. Deacon auto aims and you can't use melee or grab other bikers when you are doing those chase sequences. The only real thing that i liked and wish that we saw more of, was the hordes that you can fight, but they aren't nearly used enough honestly. All in all, this feels like one of those Ubisoft titles, but a very poorly executed one. There's not much else to do besides the main story and few objectives here and there. The game also massively stutters when you are riding the bike. Despite me having a pretty good setup. Idk why it does that. Not even tweaking the settings help with that.
The game quests in this game can be completed in almost countless different ways. This is a very under-rated game.

This is an excellent game with creative world building elements. Character design is awesome.

It must get a second game soon.

A Perfect Masterpiece.
Throughout 13 years of gaming, this is probably one of the top 3 if not THE best all-time best story-rich games I have ever played (personal opinion, not objectively).
I cannot praise this game enough. I cannot believe that "critics" thought this game was not worth it at launch.
No game has ever got me hooked, invested, and engaged with it like this one did.
If you forget you’re playing a video game while playing a video game, the developers did something right. I have never experienced such real, vulnerable, beautifully human characters in a game like this before.
Some may even say it made them cry at the end while the credits were rolling. Totally not me though, but some people :)
A story that showcases humanity at its worst and at its best. A very human story in the best way possible.
If there's one game you have to play at least once in your life, it's this one.
Oh and keep the music bar in the audio settings at max. Not only is the soundtrack amazing, but it fits with the story and events of the game perfectly.
Probably  one of the best zombie open world games. story sometimes get really good but mostly is okish.

This game is absolute bad ass. If you are looking for a novel open world game, check this out. The fighting mechanics, the guns, and the tactical decisions on consumables are all good. Get it on sale if you can in 2026...

My only complaint is that the merchant/gun buying system is a little weird, Some of the merchants are considered much better than others. The offerings should be a little more balanced.  I also find it strange that pretty much everyone the main character encounters outside of "camps" is considered to be "marauders." The character can be a bit flakey for a 1% Biker imho as well, but these are minor things.

I got to this game late, but oh my god is it an amazing game. I am absolutely furious too hear the plans they had for this game to be a complete trilogy and Sony screwed it over. I want the second and the third immediately. I'd pay fat stacks for them.

Phenomenal game and one of my favorite zombie games ever. Plus its based in the state I currently live in and have been to many of the places featured in it like Crater Lake, so bonus points for that.

Days Gone is one of the most underrated or under appreciated games I have ever seen. I slept on this game forever because people did not review it well and when I finally got around to playing it I was really impressed with how good it felt to play and how good it felt to get better at the systems that exist here. This game is not a 10/10 hit by any means but its a real solid feeling 7 or 8. It's the kind of game that you have on your shelf for when you aren't super sure what single player experience you want to hop into next. Just throw this on and forget about everything enjoy yourself. Explore. Clear infestations. Ride around on the best feeling motorcycle gaming has ever received. Clear Hordes. Oh by the way, the coolest ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ zombie hordes and horde fights I have seen in a video game to date. I really like how terrifying these hordes are. This game is not trying to be a horror shooter at all but there have been times where I was clearing infestations and being a bit loud and confident just to get swarmed by a horde because it was night time and I did not sweep my surroundings wide enough to realize that there was a horde nearby. The hordes are also very cleaver and sometimes easy to just stumble into because they all will group up incredibly close together and hunch over on the ground like they are sleeping at times and sometimes they will do this at the edge of a body of water in some zones. When this happens it is quite easy to just not realize they are there until you are full panic mode while 300 zombies run you down. It. Is. Terrifying. They also seek shelter and hibernate during the day for in game lore reasons that I will not spoil but when they are doing this you may walk into a really large town or point of interest and think its empty just to shoot your gun at one of the few zombies outside and realize oh wow this place is crawling in every inch with zombies. It really is something special. I think everyone that is a fan of zombie games should definitely try Days Gone. Just whatever you do. Do not go into it thinking it will be another installment of The Last of Us or Resident Evil. It really isn't in the same ball park but it is still really solid, really good fun. It gets 2 thumbs up from me any day.
Its local to my area, and feels real to play. The graphics are fantastic.

It’s been a while since a game left me this conflicted.
TL;DR: Days Gone is a good game buried under a pile of dumb decisions.

Dumb decisions are as follows:
1. Sam Witwer. A solid actor who usually plays freaks or antagonists. The devs decided they knew better and cast him as the protagonist. Dumb.
2. Character logic I guess. Days Gone came out in 2019, and when it comes to modern "politics" it could’ve been way worse, but that doesn’t mean it’s fine. There’s a lot of outdated cringe, but the real problem is the game is packed with nonsense like a wholesome, practically "lawful good" biker who’s somehow also a 1%er. Dumb. And then you’ve got that frat bro pretending to be "urban" (??) as the main villain. I mean of course he is, who else. Crazy on so many levels, also dumb. 
3. Story missions. They exist mostly as an excuse to interact with characters, nothing more. The actual mission design is mostly mid, but sometimes just straight-up dumb (yes, helicopter missions, I’m looking at you).
4. Balance. A game like this needs constant tension, so for me it only really worked on Survival I difficulty. Problem is, that difficulty removes fast travel, and it clearly wasn't designed to be played that way. NPCs regularly send you across the map. The world isn’t realistic, more of a big playground - which is good in its own way - but playground logic doesn’t make sense, so without GPS navigation you just get stuck constanlty in some made up obstacle or other. And on top of that, geniuses put the "show minimap' ON A COOLDOWN. Dumb.
5. Progression. In their attempt to make players feel it, devs basically crippled Deacon at the start. You begin as Gollum on a bicycle, armed with a Nerf gun (that’s the AK in this universe). Over time, both riding and shooting start to feel great, but by then a lot of players will have already dropped the game. Dumb.

Now for the good parts.
The core gameplay loop is really enjoyable. The game evolves from stealth and hit-and-run into free roam across multiple locations, and then into actively hunting zombies. Each phase feels good in its own way. On survival I, game presented reasonable challenge from start to end,.
The locations look interesting, the actors in cutscenes do their job well, engine roars and zombie hordes swarm you from all sides - good times.
Overall, free roam and horde hunting feel great (especially once you’re upgraded). But to actually get properly upgraded, you have to push through story missions. Again, dumb.

In the end, I spent 50 hours and didn’t really want it to end. I might even go clear the map, which I normally never do.

At 15 euros, I say it's worth it.

Very detailed open world, it doesn't hurt that I live in Oregon now either, so it feels closer to home so to speak. The best part? Taking on your first horde. Even better? Surviving it. If you like infected/zombie games you will dig this. Slightly slow start, but well worth the progression for both the character and the bike. Speaking of the bike, one of the best vehicles in a game I can recall in a long while. Seriously, this game got the short end of the stick on release. An unsung gem. (Played to completion on PS5, had to try it on PC.)
I disliked the endless tutorial popups that interrupt gameplay, the generic "survival" mechanics copied directly from Far Cry and the absolutely cringeworthy dialogue.

Cool game and concept.
♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ story and poor acting in special moments.

Spoilers
.
.
.
Very poor reaction when his wife sees him after 2 years thinking he is dead.

That biker wants to help the survivers, convincing their leader it's a good idea, willing to blow up the Feaks nests caves for them and then they just all forget about it and never mention it again.

That f*cking colonel respects him at first for being a veteran, he risks his life so many times for the militia, then a stranger comes from nowhere telling them he is a traitor, they listen for some reason and wanna hang him.

The captian who saved your ass from death penalty is just gone during the revolution and doesn't return to help the militia he cared so much about.

These are just few examples I had in my mind.


I don't know who's behind the game but there's a reason it will not have any continuation.
Play it if you love good apocalyptic feeling

Gameplay: 9
Story: 8
Presentation (graphics and sound): 8
Fun Factor: 9

My Score (not a pure average of the category scores above): 9.4

Playthrough Time: 80 hours

SHORT & SWEET SPOILER FREE REVIEW:

Days Gone is an AMAZING game. Does is have some issues? Yes, but a flawed game can still be amazing. This title has the best big zombie horde mechanics I've ever seen. Some truly butt clenching moments while you're being chased by massive groups of zombies where one mistake can lead to you getting absolutely dog piled. The weapon and bike upgrade system is great at giving you a constant feeling of progression and customization throughout the playthrough. Very satisfying stuff. 

The story has more to offer than I had initially assumed. While it doesn't have the deepest character development or the most profound themes it DOES a great job at portraying relationships and interactions which really made me care about the people involved. 

Overall if you want an amazing zombie experience with a great story then pick up Days Gone.

Всё хорошо, кроме 1 максимально тупорылой миссии по зачистке орды с подгорелым капитаном, логически тупо в целом в одиночку орду чистить + баги вылезают, если долго каруселить орду вокруг игровой зоны - исчезают грузовики, но коллизия никуда не уходит, 10 врезавшихся байков в невидимый грузовик из 10. Пожалуй, на этом моё прохождение заканчивается

I have 30 hours this game on PS5 still haven't finished it yet. Bought it for PC later... this is a PS4 game and i can't still believe how good Day's Gone. The game is crazy good. I'm in love with this game. 10/10 😃

Good:
- Story driven gameplay
- Nice visuals
- Standout voice acting, especially from Sam Witwer
- No issues for me with stability
- Didn't run into too many bugs
- Decently long game

Neutral:
- Would have preferred story DLC over the challenge mode that was implemented
- Multiple playthroughs required for 100% achievements

Bad:
- Some open world dialogue is cut off under certain circumstances

9/10 

One of my favorite games of all time. Its pretty slow to start but once you get past level 2 trust in Copelands camp its a lot more fun! KBM is kinda weird considering the controls are floaty so I definitely recommend controller as it feels a lot more natural whilest playing. Awesome game though, the story is amazing and the gameplay is like no other zombie game I have played before.

With my specs I do get some frame jitter out of no-where for some reason but once I had the Nvidia app optimize the settings for me it ran better. 



♥♥♥♥ THE GROUP OF PEOPLE THAT LIKES RAP AND HAPPENS TO ALSO LIVE IN LOW INCOME NEIGHBORHOODS ALSO KNOW AS "HOODS" WHILE BUYING FRIED CHICKEN AND KOOL AID WITH AN EBT CARD THAT THEY STOLE FROM A NICE GATED SUBBURB COMMUNITY!!!

(sorry my cat jumped on my keyboard)
Love the game could not stop until I was done with the story.
This is the only game I've played all the way through multiple times. Multiple levels. And I'm still playing it. I enjoy playing this game.

One of the most profound and underrated game of all time.
This is easily a re-playable game that offer a vast amount of possibilities. While the chances are slim, but I do hope to see a sequel happening for this game.
I don't understand how this game is incredibly slept on.  It's one of the best open world post-apocalyptic story driven games I've ever played.
amazing open world survival game, graphics are great, story is great. overall an absolute top 10 game of all time.

Really is a unique take on zombie games with a RDR-style open world setting. Very immersive locations and excellent gameplay.

Also got serious whiplash when Arkantos' va shows up in the second half of the game lol (oh spoilers).

I found it a little confusing at first, like when i would complete a mission it would say something like 12%. After a few hours and upgrading my gear I really got into it. Beautiful graphics, good story, lots of things to do to keep you busy. Who doesn't enjoy killing hordes of freakers.
Interesting, fun game with a nice dosage of action. I love every second of it.
clap zombies, sick story, game is rad

This game is an underrated Mater piece. This is a great game for sure. Great story, Detailed world, good mechanics. And the remaster just makes it that much better. I started this game on PS4 back in the day. Didn't get to finish it for whatever reason or other. I bought this remaster about a year ago. I finally pulled it out of my back log of games. I have been addicted to this since I started 2 days ago. Very good game!!!!

It is very VERY long, but the combat is good, taking down the bigger things have clever solutions and the story is some of the best i've seen out of a game. 

A tier open world game.

This was a hell of a fight! This is absolute masterpiece! 
Everything is simply brilliant: the gameplay, the storyline, the acting, and oh my goodness, the music!
The game is absolutely marvellous, right down to the tiniest detail. 
Don't even think about giving this project a miss - it's well worth it.
It was a wonderful experience and I'll be returning to it time and time again with great pleasure!

The world woud also be a little better if we could see a worthy sequel to this story and its incredible characters!

I absolutely love this game. I had it for PS4 and was close to the end, but I was unable to finish it because the console bricked. This version is just as good, if not better because of the Broken Road DLC. I did have a one glitch in my 10 hours so far where I go activate a NERO facility that requires a fuse would not unlock. Doing a quick save at the bike and then reloading fixed that for me. Besides that, no complaints from me.
Like games that are character and plot driven.  Good graphics don't hurt either!
Fantastic game.  Game play, depth of story, replayability. variety of approaches, freestyle open world done perfectly.  We need a sequel.

Great game, good graphics, wonderful world. Overall storyline is great but main character can be a little annoying. I have played this multiple times sense its release and it does not get old. Still a great game in 2026.

It's a great game that has a slow start, but then ramps up quite a bit. If you love zombies and survival games, this is worth a playthrough. One day, I can only hope we will get surprised by a 2nd game.

It is so fun to play this game while it's raining and thundering outside brings back that cozy feeling when you used to watch your favourite show back in the day. And a classic zombie open RPG game who doesn't love that. I wish they made a sequel

Fantastic game, it's quality and story make me nostalgic for the epic AAA games we used to have. I started playing this because of Travis Ryan, but I actually stuck around and finished the whole story and most of the side content. What impressed me the most is that I actually wanted to.

Game runs well and has very few bugs at all. 

At certain times, you need to be careful, otherwise you might cancel out some radio communications with others if you go into a mission zone or cutscene, and I don't know if there's a way to hear them again besides reloading. I would just recommend not rushing, and not moving to a new area until you finish the dialog.
Very good game, nice if you like open world games. We need a days gone 2!!!
unreasonable hate, the game is amazing, good movement, cool bike, nice weapons, hooligan virus
starts out a little slow but turns into an amazing game with plenty of play time.

I just think its fun!!! story line is good, love the freakers they are so fun to fight. Really enjoy the open world where you can hunt and gather plants to earn money/trust, upgrading your bike is also so cool. Demolshing hordes with all the cool guns and gadest you get is so much fuin

The game itself is to die for. The fact it does not have a sequel and TLOU does is genuinely criminal. This games story and mechanics are so incredibly well designed now more than ever, the games issue lies with the damn shooting. If I'm shooting a freaker in the head he shoulDN'T BE ABLE TO DODGE A WHOLE BULLET MOVING AT SUPER SONIC SPEEDS. Thats all the game is genuinely 10/10.

Fun game. Wish they had made a Days Gone 2. So much they could have built upon and improved, so much lost potential. I am biased though.. storyline got to me lol. Nevertheless, a top tier single player game ; I like to call it brother simulator. Whole time taking care of Boozer and his loyalty didn't fade. I tell you, THAT is what a brother is. Something I will NEVER find out apparently. LOL
Best ever game after so long ,so much good story .graphics ,character build up ,audio,weapons,horde every dam thing is good enough to experiece best
One of the best games not only in the zombie genre, but in its genre overall. The story, gameplay, atmosphere, and graphics are all amazingly done. Days Gone is truly a hidden gem
Awesome game. Story, gameplay, graphics, art style you name it. A shame Sony is letting this IP rot.

Amazing Game! Not going lie, after 8hrs or so in, I dropped it, not because it was a bad game, it was because it was kinda slow and I figured it was just another zombie game. But after they released a remaster I figured Id give it a try to see whats new. Once the story opened up I couldnt put down this game. 62 hrs later, I got all the guns, my bike is so specked out i wish it was real and one of the most beautiful character/characters driven stories ive ever played. Tears came to my eyes on multiple occasions. The actors did amazing! Highly Recommended
This game is one of the best games ever made. The story is incredible, the gameplay is extremely fun, and there is a large variety of weapons you can use.
I like. Starkiller my guy. Story kind of funky at times but overall good. Looks and runs good which is better than most 2025-2026 released titles.
Days Gone is such a Beautiful Zombie game, just Perfect. The game and Story is creative and the characters are Peak. This game is so Good that I watched all the credits at the End.

One of the better zombie open world games I've played.  The story is fun, the game-play is fun, and best of all it's located in the best state in the USA -- Oregon!

It gameplay has just enough depth and options to be interesting, without becoming tedious and overwhelming.

The amount of glazing this game gets from its fans is frankly embarrassing, this game isn't the second coming of Jesus Christ it's an open world game about zombies. There are a billion games like it. This game is certainly better than the vast majority of those other games but we don't usually unconditionally praise things for being better than average last time I recalled. The gunplay is fun and the story is enough to engage you. It's good. It gets a little repetitive at toward the end but so does almost every other open-world game. The UI is weird and uncomfortable and I don't like it. The enemy scaling is non-existent, the better weapons you get the game only gets easier with only minor different enemy subtypes that amount to "shoot them more". Again, like almost every other open-world RPG that has ever existed.

Again, the issues with the game are perfectly normal for an average open-world experience. This game doesn't do anything unique and anybody who glazes this game like it's their lost dad who came back with the cigarettes needs a reality check. It's competent and worth it's price.
This was a great game and a great story.  Gameplay was good.  A sequel would of been nice, so here's hope that they do release one.
Liked the open world and the feel of just hopping on your bike to traverse the map. The story was also interesting and longer than expectet.
good game but i got stuck on a horde mission and gave up, still i like it u should buy it.

honestly a very good game. characters and soundtrack are great, gameplay is fun. the environment is beautiful to drive through, too. quite an emotional story!
plus, deacon is cute 10/10
Edit: I am 48 hours into the game and I feel like there's plenty of story left. you get your money's worth

Amazing game enjoyed the entire story and the gameplay of it was phenomonal really hope sony pulls their heads outta their butts and makes Days Gone 2 and just let their games be on PC console wars is over and has been for a very long time
yes i love this game. i always wanted to play this game finally got it. It has amazing storyline and feel every jump from the walkers.

Great game with great gaming Storyline Good graphics and and flexible gameplay only bad part about this game is it can be buggy somtiems and there's no second part of this game coming rest is magnificent

Es un juego muy disfrutalbe, aunque su historia es simple, las mecanicas son faciles de entender, en lo personal disfruté mucho de este juego pues me parecía muy relajante, debido a su ambientación en la naturaleza. Espero que salga la secuela
10/10 GRAPHICS ARE GOOD the optimization is grate but bend studio please make the playing all night mission easier i planed on playing the entire game but i am stuck on the breaker part

This game has one of my favorite story lines of all time!!! I would recommend this game on that alone. I have no complaints with the graphics, especially since this is a couple years old now, my 10 year old PC could run this game at its max with no issues at all. As for the survival and PvE aspects, its not the best. There are a lot of times where doing all of the side missions can feel repetitive and boring. Also it was very easy to end up with more money than I could spend which made the economy just broken. 

Regardless, this game was never a PvE zombie shooter, it was a story game and it did its job very well. 9.3/10

This is honestly a really great zombie game to play, The pacing is geat and slowly draws you in & has simple but believable world building, The story is pretty well put together and has tons of moments that leave you in awe, It does however have some problem, I've noticed in the survival challenges that you are not able to progress some of the challenges specfically the races as they dont spawn all the checkpoints neccessary to complete the race, Albeit this it still has some elemnts worth exploring but for 100% completion it may be hard for some 7/10
A really great game, unfortunately it will never get a sequel, but I still highly recommend playing it.

After many years of having this game, I finally play it. A Buggy Mess on Linux, But a lovely mess of a zombie game. I shouldn't like this game. I don't like extra stress. I don't like playing horror game. I don't like bikers. A few hours in, I like almost everything about this game which is a zombie game playing as a biker. 

By buggy mess, I mean on my Linux PC. I run PikaOS Niri. The game tends to not start normally. Doesn't like to be in full screen. Most of the time if start from Steam, it will end up with Keyboard not working. I played it last year and menu wheel doesn't work so I couldn't get pass the tutorial about crafting bandage. Now, it still have trouble starting, but when it does start and keyboard works, everything is smooth and fun.

This is a good zombie game that hold up really well in 2026, assuming you can manage the bug or not running into one.
Finished the entire game in VR with the Luke Ross mod. Would do it again. Super fun

Good game, some missions can get bit tedious cause its so repetitive with the bounty hunter missions and just clearing camp after camp, but clearing out hordes are very entertaining 

Story line is really good, bit dull at the start of the game but it progressively gets better the further you get and in my opinion peaks late mid game 

Worth playing 100%

Going through my backlog and this game was on it and boy am I disappointed I waited so long to finish this game. This game is a solid 7/10 but defiantly worth a playthrough. Few gripes ive had is stuff like human npcs are super dumb but the zombies are pretty smart. I tried a few times to bait zombies to doing the dirty work for me but both zombies and humans teamed up against me. Story was pretty good everyone hopes to love someone like the main character and his wife love each other. It is a shame we wont get a second one.
I loved this game. Cool plot and mechanics. You can feel thrill of being chased by freaks while your motocykle is far away or broken down 😅

Its a shame we will never get a sequel. Ive put so many hours into this game on my PlayStation. Im slowly moving over to PC and im glad that this game is available on here. For those who are interested in this game, it is a slow burn in the beginning. About 2 hours in is when things start picking up and the story starts branching out into multiple storylines. But even then the beginning parts of the game have enough to peak your interest and keep you invested in whats happening. If you're still not sure about buying it wait for a sell, but at full price this game is 100% worth the price and your time.
Fantastic game, captavating story, superb voice acting from the whole cast but especially the always impressive Sam Witwer, and incredibly satisfying and fun gameplay. Would love a sequel.

**Days Gone is a game that pisses you off, but somehow still works.**

It’s like a rusty bike cobbled together from both good and bad parts. The economy creaks, the side quests drag on, some mechanics feel unfinished, and in places you can clearly see the exposed seams of cut content: here, there was obviously meant to be a choice; there, a harsher survival system; and this whole mechanic seems to have survived only because there wasn’t enough time to rip it out by the roots.

But then the game throws a horde at you, or puts you back on your bike, and somehow it all starts to click. The roar of the engine, the screech of Freakers behind you, and the panicked improvisation with your last few bullets give it exactly the kind of raw nerve that makes you willing to forgive the rust.

And for God’s sake, don’t start the game on Normal. Go straight for Hard. The game lets you lower the difficulty, but not raise it, and Normal here feels more like Easy.

Days Gone Review — 72 Hours In

After 72 hours with Days Gone, I can honestly say this game pulled me in way more than I expected. The immersion is incredible. The world feels dangerous, lonely, and alive all at the same time. Riding through the open roads, managing fuel, listening for Freakers, and never fully knowing what is around the next bend creates a constant sense of tension.

The storyline is one of the strongest parts of the experience. Deacon’s journey, the relationships, the camps, and the slow reveal of what happened all kept me wanting to push forward. It is not just another zombie game. It has heart, loss, loyalty, and survival all wrapped together in a way that makes the characters feel real.

What really surprised me is how intense the game can be. There are moments where your heart rate genuinely climbs, especially when a horde suddenly notices you or when you are low on supplies and trying to escape by inches. Few games manage to create that kind of pressure so naturally.

The open world is also beautifully built. It rewards exploration without feeling empty, and the motorcycle adds a personal connection to the journey. Your bike is not just transportation. It feels like your lifeline.

After spending 72 hours in this world, I can say Days Gone is an amazing survival action game with great atmosphere, memorable characters, tense gameplay, and a story that keeps you invested. It deserves far more praise than it gets.

Highly recommended for anyone who enjoys open-world survival games with strong storytelling and real tension.
Better to run it on a GPU with 6GB or higher GPU , Otherwise its going to stutter
It's a pretty good game nothing much I can really say as it would be for those who enjoy the zombie genre

Days Gone is one of my all time favorite games. The story, character arcs, art, and direction that this game takes is unbelievable. its a game i come back to every other year and play. This most recent playthrough, I did go ahead and take on all ambushes, nero, horde and other such side quests than I normally would do.  I do recommend this to literally anyone who loves any form of zombies. the fight/play style, vast of guns, unlockables and ability to path around zombies makes this game as unique as it is. 
One of... if not.. the best open world zombie game of all time. Bend Studios... make a part 2 :D

Used supplies mod to get through, but this is still one of the best games I have ever played. The story and storytelling are fantastic. The game does an incredible job of making you feel hopeless and later offer hope and make you fight hard.
games great runs perfect i remember being at the last mission of this game and forgetting all about it and ended up selling the console i played it on so getting to reinstall it in play again is fun

Playing Days Gone was honestly a great experience for me.

I got hooked pretty quickly - the world feels alive, riding the bike around is super satisfying, and upgrading it made me actually care about it. The atmosphere is amazing, especially at night or when you’re just cruising through empty roads and forests.

The physics actually add a lot too, the bike has weight, you can feel the difference on dirt vs road, and it’s easy to mess up if you go too fast or brake badly. It makes riding feel more real instead of just arcade style.

The story surprised me, and honestly almost every task felt enjoyable.

And the hordes? Easily one of the coolest parts (pure chaos, but super fun).

Overall, I had a really good time with it and didn’t want it to end. Definitely worth playing (and yeah, I’m still waiting for a sequel… if it ever happens).

I never got to recommending this game. While some of the gameplay leaves something to be desired and doesnt really get you pulled into the world itself. The writing is magnificent. I love Deacon. He's an actual interesting protagonist. His connections and who he is really seems to matter in the game. They didnt just make him a rough and tough kill all biker guy or a sterotype. Hes a nihilistic sometimes, realist others and yet someone with passion and kindness in droves. His emotions get the better of him and his buddy Boozer sometimes in negative ways but it also show the love they have for those around them and the world they live in. They are two guys i would wanna be stuck in an apocolypse with. 

The gameplay is just ok. Nothing new and sometimes trips over itself like when trying to climb a small rock and you just... can't. Very video gamey. You feel the limiations everywhere. Which i feel like we will continue to see less of as new games come out and this won't age the best in that sense. Almost feels like a last of its era type of game.

a bit clunky but the horror is good and the world war z hordes are pretty thrilling to fight. The novelty does eventually wear off and by the end, game feels kind of underwhelming. If you know what you're looking for and what you're looking at, this is the game.

Due to PlayStation Studios’ decision to stop porting single-player games to PC — officially confirmed by CEO Hermen Hulst — I can no longer recommend this title or any other games from PlayStation Publishing and its affiliated studios

This is the most mid game i have ever played. It's not bad but its definitely not good either. Deacon is the most insufferable ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ that makes for a poor excuse of a main character. I would have rather played as boozer. Also Sarah's big reveal was mega retarded and literally went nowhere. It was just shoehorned into the story because why not?? Same thing with "Carlos" to a lesser extent. The best part of this game is Jack...the dog that you see for like 2 minutes in the whole game. Play this game if you want to play video game brainrot.

This game is really of poor quality, i don't know how to explain it other than it feels "barebones" and overall unoriginal. The Upgrade system feels copy and pasted from any other UE5 or maybe a Ubislop game. 

Despite being a large part of the game the combat while riding a bike is extremely underwhelming since deacon will just auto aim at enemies, You'd think they'd let you hit enemies with melee weapons while on a bike in the zombie apocalypse biker game. I mean seriously it'd be awesome to whack zombies heads off with a tire iron, huge lost opportunity.

The guns also feel like absolute pea shooters with all the enemies being somewhat tanky bullet sponges. Without head shots they have a pretty high time to kill, this leads to me taking out most of my encounters with a rifle at long range. 

This game kind of feels like a Far-Cry game trying to be RDR2.

Just beat it, writing this fresh.

As much as I enjoyed my time after a certain point, the game suffers from trying to be long enough to justify its' existence. While as I was nearing the end of the game, I had to look up how many missions were left because the story and game felt like it was going absolutely no where. I think the number was something like 150 MAIN missions.. And I felt every one of those missions.

After about 20 hours, I decided to stop doing side stuff because they ended up all being the same: "this guy left our camp, go kill him," or "rescue this dude." Whatever it may have been. There was ONE story that actually paid off and it was Lisa's but there was so much filler I just had to step away from it. I felt like I invested too much time in the game and didn't want to stop playing because the side missions were so pointless. I got back on track and that really didn't help much once the main story picked up again...

I really don't care for love stories. In entertainment, they're always extremely unbelievable and ridden with questionable decisions. I won't spoil anything but I thought Deacon and Sarah's love story was stupid and nothing special. I just started skipping Deacon's flashbacks with Sarah because they were doing absolutely nothing in getting me invested in their life. 

I'm glad we don't live in a time anymore where an open world game or any kind of single player experiences feels like it needs to waste your time in order to justify its' price tag. I REALLY don't mind paying $60/$70 for a single player experience that's 8-12 hours, as long as it's worth it. Adding stupid side quests that go no where or telling the player they can go "explore" and it just being up the same thing over and over again just to make the game 20+ hours is STUPID. I don't care about doing menial tasks that end up taking hours just so I can get some dumb new weapon type or  new weapon or whatever. It's a nice thing to add to your game but it shouldn't be part of the core gameplay. A lot happens in this game, the writers when ♥♥♥♥♥♥ crazy on this story but I can't help but imagine how great this game would've been if it was just a nicely packaged 15-20 hour experience.

I'm glad I beat it, this game has been sitting in my backlog for years and I was always told it was an underrated game. In my opinion, it's overrated. It's a shallow experience, with awful voice acting (mainly from Deacon), and an overtly drawn out story that was not satisfying to conclude.

Anyway, on to the next...

This is a deceptively bad game. If you watch someone else playing it, it looks like a perfectly fine third-person shooter, but when you actually play it, it overwhelms you with a lot of annoyances.

It feels like everything in the game is built around a few equally questionable assumptions:
- Do it because other open-world games do it. It feels like a checklist of what a modern AAA open-world game is supposed to include.
- Do it because everything in games needs a progression system attached to it.
- Do it because it’s more realistic.
And these ideas are followed blindly, even when the end result clearly hurts the experience more than it helps it.

But first, the good elements:
- The game is technically well realized. Even though I encountered a few (quite immersion-breaking) bugs, overall it looks polished.
- I liked the characters. They are believable and well written, though their interactions aren't nearly as good.
- I enjoyed a couple of standout story moments.

That’s about it. Because for the rest, the game is extremely irritating. I’ll try to list the main issues that come to mind:
- Weapons lack punch, and shooting doesn’t feel satisfying. Early on this is even worse, because shooting ability is tied to the progression system. Was it necessary? Probably not, as buying more powerful weapons with money would probably have been enough.
- Low stamina and low health are needlessly punishing at the beginning for the same reason.
- Autosaves are often poorly placed. You quickly learn to manually save at your bike whenever possible, but even that doesn’t always help; sometimes you take a break only to find yourself 10–20 minutes further back than expected.
- Driving doesn’t feel great either, which is not ideal in a game where riding your bike is central. Again, bike upgrades are locked behind progression, and the bike itself is deliberately unresponsive, likely in the name of realism. The result is a slow, clunky, unfun ride for a large portion of the game.
- Fast travel has unnecessary restrictions. You can’t use it unless you’re very far from enemies, and you can’t use it if there are enemy camps between you and your destination. It’s realism for its own sake, but it doesn’t meaningfully improve the experience. And when you do fast travel, you spawn standing next to your bike instead of riding on it, adding another pointless step.
- The UI is poor: badly organized and unpleasant to navigate.
- Almost every interaction uses hold inputs instead of simple button presses, which makes even basic looting feel tedious.
- Like many open-world games, it’s filled with repetitive quest structures and recycled activities that become boring after the second time you complete them. It baffles me how developers can think "...then the player is going to have to walk in circles for 5 minutes looking for a gas can, then fill up the generator, then destroy all the speakers, then maybe walk in circles another couple of minutes to find the fuse, then enter the mobile unit and then finally find the power-up. Fine. Now let's make them do all of that 30 more times, they are gonna love it!".
- Quest pacing is also padded. After finishing a mission, the next one from the same character doesn’t activate immediately; instead, you wait for a call that triggers it.
- There are slow, unskippable animations during loading that show the time of day changing, which quickly become annoying.
- Enemy variety is limited. Wolves, in particular, are frustrating, often appearing at the worst possible moments, they are fast and have QTE grabs that interrupt gameplay. Meanwhile, hordes, arguably the most interesting feature, are introduced too late.
- Dialogue timing is poor and often gets interrupted by other gameplay events.
- The bike constantly needs fuel and maintenance. In a game built around riding a motorcycle, stopping every few minutes to refuel becomes tedious very quickly. Once again, upgrades that reduce this friction are locked behind progression. Early game travel also frequently forces long rides, making the issue worse.
- Ammo scarcity is another early frustration. Even with careful aim and headshots, you can run dry mid-mission. As expected, this is also tied to progression upgrades.
- Vendor interactions feel sluggish.
- Some story segments feel unnecessary and drag the pacing down, like the monologues at Sarah’s tomb where the protagonist rehashes recent events, or the Colonel’s lengthy speeches later on.
- The story itself isn’t strong. It feels like a collection of loosely connected ideas rather than a cohesive narrative, and it frequently falls back on standard zombie-apocalypse tropes.

Overall, Days Gone is less a bad game than an irritating one, but it irritated me enough that I can’t recommend it. It could have been significantly better if the developers had focused on refining what they already had instead of trying to replicate every open-world convention. And instead of a pointless remaster, it might have been more worthwhile to improve some of the game weakest systems.

[Leaving Mission Area]

I love when a supposed open world game restricts how I can approach missions.

The bike is a liability, not an asset. You constantly have to take care of it, and it’s mostly just used to move faster.

The game is full of quick-time events. Instead of leaving me in control of my character, it constantly interrupts: press the right key or die.

So much wasted potential. The graphics are good, the ambience is decent, the premise is interesting.

But I hate this kind of gameplay where you cannot choose how you approach and solve problems.

The AI is just stupid. The only interesting enemies are humans, and they just line up, all passing through the same point so you can headshot them one by one with a pistol.

Despite a lot of potential for an interesting story, the characters feel shallow and uninteresting. I don’t know if it’s the writing, the voice acting, or both, but I feel no connection to what the game wants me to do.

If you like zombie games where everything is heavily guided, you might enjoy it. Otherwise, expect to be disappointed.

Days Gone feels like a low-budget Ubisoft-style open world game. Outside of the Horde mechanics, there’s honestly not much to do. Most missions are just riding from point A to point B while barely anything happens. The story gets better near the end, and a few missions are actually pretty fun, but it takes way too long to get there. The remastered version is also badly optimized. Riding the bike causes constant stuttering even on a decent PC.

The game is alright to have fun. Story isn't great and the main draw is the gameplay. Shooting is pleasant and driving the bike is pleasant. Biggest issue is the unskippable credits. If you quit during the 30 minute long credits, at least on pc, when you launch the game again, you have to replay the last mission. This is so disrespectful to a person's free time, that I won't recommend playing this. I regret buying it. It ruined the whole experience.
I can't play the game on PC anymore it crushes down at startup. It was fine until Wednesday 13 2026. I do not recommend the game since it was retired and left with bugs and unsolvable problems.

Planned to abandon the game, Game ends on major cliffhanger, Instead of adding a prequel DLC or any form of real content, Years after claiming the game wont get a sequel, they sell you a horde mode that you could probably just find on NMM... Idk whats going on in the gaming industry but they need to put down the st*pid juice
Trying to defeat the first boss at the plane crash site, I didn't feel it was worth the stress so uninstalled.  Won't buying any sequels.

Overall the game is meh. It needs a ton of polish. The motorcycle just upshifts infinitely once you hit top speed, some cutscenes start and end out of nowhere and so many other small but obvious details like how a motorcycle works that just make you feel like the developers didn't care at all. After 14 hours, I can't take it anymore.
The last act of the game is so boring that i couldn't take it anymore and just uninstalled it without finishing it.
